title: Lindisfarne Raid
description: The Viking raid on Lindisfarne monastery marked the beginning of the Viking Age in Britain. Norse warriors descended on the holy island, shocking the Christian world.

title: Siege of Paris
description: A Viking fleet of over 700 ships sailed up the Seine and besieged Paris for nearly a year, extracting a massive ransom from the Frankish king.

title: Battle of Stamford Bridge
description: King Harald Hardrada of Norway was defeated by Harold Godwinson of England, effectively ending the Viking Age of invasion.

title: Leif Erikson reaches Vinland
description: Norse explorer Leif Erikson became the first European to reach North America, landing at a place he called Vinland.

title: Sack of Nantes
description: Vikings sailed up the Loire river and sacked the city of Nantes, killing the bishop and massacring the clergy gathered for a feast day.

title: Settlement of Iceland
description: Norse settlers led by Ingólfr Arnarson established the first permanent settlement in Iceland, marking a major expansion of the Viking world.

title: Battle of Hafrsfjord
description: Harald Fairhair defeated rival Norwegian kings in a decisive naval battle, unifying Norway under a single ruler for the first time.

title: Rollo granted Normandy
description: Viking leader Rollo was granted the region of Normandy by the Frankish king Charles the Simple, founding what would become the Duchy of Normandy.

title: Vikings discover Greenland
description: Erik the Red, exiled from Iceland, sailed west and discovered Greenland, establishing the first Norse settlements there.

title: Battle of Clontarf
description: Irish High King Brian Boru defeated a Viking and Irish alliance near Dublin, breaking Viking power in Ireland. Brian Boru was killed in his tent after the battle.

title: Varangian Guard founded
description: Byzantine Emperor Basil II formed the elite Varangian Guard, composed largely of Norse and later Anglo-Saxon warriors who served as his personal bodyguard.

title: Raid on Seville
description: A large Viking fleet entered the Guadalquivir river and attacked Seville, one of the most surprising Viking raids deep into Muslim Al-Andalus.

title: Foundation of the Ottoman Empire
description: Osman I declared independence from the Seljuk Sultanate of Rum, establishing the Ottoman dynasty that would last over 600 years.

title: Fall of Constantinople
description: Sultan Mehmed II conquered Constantinople, ending the Byzantine Empire and transforming the city into the Ottoman capital Istanbul.

title: Battle of Mohács
description: The Ottomans under Suleiman the Magnificent defeated the Kingdom of Hungary, opening the way for Ottoman expansion into Central Europe.

title: Siege of Vienna
description: The Ottoman army led by Kara Mustafa Pasha besieged Vienna but was repelled by a combined Christian force, marking the turning point of Ottoman expansion in Europe.

title: Battle of Kosovo
description: Ottoman forces defeated the Serbian-led coalition, establishing Ottoman dominance over the Balkans. Prince Lazar of Serbia was killed, making him a martyr in Serbian culture.

title: Conquest of Egypt
description: Sultan Selim I defeated the Mamluk Sultanate and conquered Egypt, bringing the holy cities of Mecca and Medina under Ottoman control.

title: Battle of Lepanto
description: A Holy League fleet defeated the Ottoman navy in the Gulf of Patras, ending the myth of Ottoman naval invincibility in the Mediterranean.

title: Suleiman takes Belgrade
description: Suleiman the Magnificent captured Belgrade, the gateway to Central Europe, opening the road for further Ottoman expansion northward.

title: Conquest of Rhodes
description: Suleiman the Magnificent expelled the Knights of St. John from Rhodes after a long siege, removing a major obstacle to Ottoman naval dominance.

title: Battle of Ankara
description: Timur the Great defeated and captured Sultan Bayezid I, plunging the Ottoman Empire into a civil war known as the Ottoman Interregnum.

title: Conquest of Algiers
description: Ottoman admiral Hayreddin Barbarossa captured Algiers from the Spanish, extending Ottoman influence across North Africa.

title: Conquest of Crimea
description: The Ottoman Empire established its protectorate over the Crimean Khanate, extending its reach deep into Eastern Europe and controlling the northern Black Sea coast.

title: Invasion of Poland
description: Germany invaded Poland on September 1, 1939, triggering declarations of war from Britain and France and beginning World War II.

title: Battle of Stalingrad
description: One of the bloodiest battles in human history. The Soviet defense of Stalingrad turned the tide of the war on the Eastern Front.

title: D-Day — Normandy Landings
description: Allied forces launched the largest seaborne invasion in history on the beaches of Normandy, beginning the liberation of Western Europe.

title: Battle of Midway
description: A decisive naval battle in the Pacific where the United States defeated Japan, destroying four Japanese carriers and shifting the balance of the Pacific War.

title: Fall of France
description: German forces swept through the Ardennes and outflanked the Maginot Line, forcing France to surrender in just six weeks.

title: Battle of Britain
description: The Royal Air Force defeated the German Luftwaffe in the first major campaign fought entirely by air forces, preventing a German invasion of Britain.

title: Operation Barbarossa
description: Germany launched the largest military invasion in history against the Soviet Union, opening the Eastern Front and ultimately sealing Germany's fate.

title: Attack on Pearl Harbor
description: Japan launched a surprise military strike on the US naval base at Pearl Harbor, Hawaii, bringing the United States into World War II.

title: Battle of El Alamein
description: British forces under Montgomery defeated Rommel's Afrika Korps, turning the tide of the North African campaign.

title: Battle of Kursk
description: The largest tank battle in history. Soviet forces repelled a massive German armored offensive, permanently seizing the strategic initiative on the Eastern Front.

title: Liberation of Paris
description: Allied and Free French forces liberated Paris from German occupation, with General de Gaulle leading a triumphant march down the Champs-Élysées.

title: Battle of Berlin
description: Soviet forces stormed the German capital in brutal street-by-street fighting. Hitler committed suicide in his bunker as the city fell, ending the war in Europe.

title: Atomic Bombing of Hiroshima
description: The United States dropped the first atomic bomb ever used in warfare on the Japanese city of Hiroshima, killing an estimated 80,000 people instantly.

title: Dunkirk Evacuation
description: Over 330,000 Allied troops were evacuated from the beaches of Dunkirk under German fire, a miraculous rescue that kept Britain in the war.

title: Founding of Rome
description: According to Roman tradition, Romulus founded the city of Rome, beginning one of history's greatest civilizations.

title: Battle of Cannae
description: Hannibal's Carthaginian army inflicted one of Rome's worst defeats, killing over 50,000 Roman soldiers. The battle is still studied in military academies today.

title: Caesar crosses the Rubicon
description: Julius Caesar led his army across the Rubicon river, an act of treason that triggered a civil war and ultimately led to the end of the Roman Republic.

title: Fall of the Western Roman Empire
description: Romulus Augustulus, the last emperor of the Western Roman Empire, was deposed by the Germanic chieftain Odoacer, ending 1,200 years of Roman rule in the west.

title: Punic Wars Begin
description: Rome and Carthage clashed in the First Punic War over control of Sicily, beginning a series of conflicts that would ultimately destroy Carthage and make Rome master of the Mediterranean.

title: Battle of Zama
description: Scipio Africanus decisively defeated Hannibal at Zama, ending the Second Punic War and establishing Rome as the dominant power in the western Mediterranean.

title: Destruction of Carthage
description: After the Third Punic War, Rome razed Carthage to the ground, salting the earth. North Africa became a Roman province.

title: Assassination of Julius Caesar
description: Julius Caesar was assassinated on the Ides of March by a group of senators including Brutus and Cassius, plunging Rome into another civil war.

title: Battle of Actium
description: Octavian defeated the combined forces of Mark Antony and Cleopatra in a naval battle, becoming the sole ruler of Rome and ending the Roman Republic.

title: Augustus becomes first Emperor
description: Octavian was granted the title Augustus by the Senate, becoming the first Emperor of Rome and beginning the Pax Romana, two centuries of relative peace.

title: Eruption of Vesuvius
description: Mount Vesuvius erupted, burying the cities of Pompeii and Herculaneum under volcanic ash and preserving them for nearly 2,000 years.

title: Construction of Hadrian's Wall
description: Emperor Hadrian ordered the construction of a wall across northern Britain to mark the northern limit of the Roman Empire and defend against barbarian raids.

title: Edict of Milan
description: Emperor Constantine and Licinius issued the Edict of Milan, granting religious tolerance throughout the Roman Empire and legitimizing Christianity.

title: Sack of Rome by Visigoths
description: Visigoth king Alaric sacked Rome for three days — the first time the city had been sacked in 800 years, shocking the Roman world.

title: Mustafa Kemal born in Thessaloniki
description: Mustafa Kemal, the future founder of the Turkish Republic, was born in Thessaloniki (then part of the Ottoman Empire). His birth marked the beginning of a life that would reshape an entire nation.

title: Gallipoli Campaign — Çanakkale
description: Allied forces launched a massive amphibious assault on the Gallipoli peninsula to open a sea route to Russia. Ottoman forces under Mustafa Kemal heroically repelled the invasion. 'I am not ordering you to attack. I am ordering you to die.' — Kemal's legendary order to his men at Chunuk Bair.

title: Armistice of Mudros
description: The Ottoman Empire signed the Armistice of Mudros, ending its participation in World War I. Allied forces occupied Istanbul, and the empire began to be carved up by the victorious powers.

title: Mustafa Kemal lands at Samsun
description: Mustafa Kemal landed at Samsun on May 19, 1919 — a date celebrated as the beginning of the Turkish War of Independence. He immediately began organizing resistance against the Allied occupation.

title: Battle of İnönü
description: Turkish nationalist forces under İsmet İnönü defeated the Greek army at İnönü, boosting morale and proving the viability of the independence movement.

title: Battle of Sakarya
description: In a 22-day battle along the Sakarya river, Mustafa Kemal personally commanded Turkish forces and halted the Greek advance deep into Anatolia. He was awarded the rank of Marshal and the title Gazi.

title: Great Offensive — Büyük Taarruz
description: Mustafa Kemal launched a massive offensive that shattered the Greek front in just days. Turkish forces swept across western Anatolia, ultimately liberating İzmir and ending the Greek presence in Asia Minor.

title: Proclamation of the Turkish Republic
description: On October 29, 1923, the Grand National Assembly proclaimed the Republic of Turkey. Mustafa Kemal became the first President, declaring: 'Ne mutlu Türküm diyene' — How happy is the one who says I am a Turk.

title: Treaty of Lausanne
description: The Treaty of Lausanne established the borders of modern Turkey and was the only treaty after WWI negotiated as an equal by a defeated power. It replaced the humiliating Treaty of Sèvres.

title: Atatürk's Alphabet Reform
description: Atatürk personally traveled across Turkey teaching the new Latin-based Turkish alphabet, replacing the Arabic script. Within months, an entire nation transitioned to a new writing system.

title: Death of Atatürk
description: Mustafa Kemal Atatürk passed away at Dolmabahçe Palace in Istanbul at 9:05 AM on November 10, 1938. To this day, Turkey observes a moment of silence at that exact time every year on November 10.
